ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite fast-food set meal comprising a deli-meat submarine sandwich, deep-fried chicken nuggets, and a beverage. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is used as the closest broader anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Sandwich) is used as the closest defensible anchor. No composite meal code exists for a sandwich + nuggets + drink set in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite fast-food meal product.

serving.description: 1 set meal (6-inch sandwich, 5 pc nuggets, 1 drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal consisting of a 6-inch Cold Cut Trio submarine sandwich, 5 pieces of deep-fried chicken nuggets, and one regular soft drink, weighing approximately 650 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Subway Cold Cut Trio 6-inch sandwich (Branded Foods) and chicken nuggets composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Subway Cold Cut Trio 6-inch sandwich and USDA SR Legacy chicken nuggets (deep-fried), combined with a standard sugar-sweetened soft drink component, weighted to a 650 g composite meal. Per-100 g values from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are retained where consistent with USDA-derived estimates. Singapore HPB Foodo database was consulted for contextual cross-check; no exact match was found for this composite meal. Input values are accepted as the working basis given USDA alignment within expected ranges.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite Subway Cold Cut Trio + nuggets + soft drink meal. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) white submarine bread GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database, white bread analogous), (2) soft drink GI ~63 (cola-type SSB, University of Sydney GI Database), (3) chicken nuggets GI ~46 (deep-fried battered chicken, analogous estimate), weighted by carbohydrate contribution across components. Composite meal GI estimated at ~55 (low). GL per serving = GI × (carbohydrate_g per serving / 100) = 55 × (90.0 / 100) = 49.7, where carbohydrate per serving = 13.85 g/100g × 650 g / 100 = 90.0 g. Note: GI 55 is borderline medium/low, but for a composite meal with significant protein and fat, it is categorized as low.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium from processed deli meats and soft drink; 276.9 mg sodium per 100 g equates to approximately 1,800 mg sodium per 650 g serving, approaching or exceeding the 2,000 mg daily limit in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal due to very high sodium from turkey bologna, ham, and salami; if consumed, request no added sauces, substitute the soft drink with water, and limit portion to half a serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ~49.7 per serving) driven by refined white bread and sugar-sweetened soft drink, with low dietary fibre (1.23 g/100 g) providing insufficient glucose absorption modulation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this meal in its standard form; substituting wholemeal bread, eliminating the soft drink in favour of water, and replacing deep-fried nuggets with grilled chicken would substantially reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combined burden of saturated fat (1.54 g/100 g), trans fat (0.08 g/100 g), high sodium (~1,800 mg per serving), and ultra-processed deli meats containing sodium nitrates, all of which are independently associated with elevated cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal due to its high sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and processed meat content; if consumed occasionally, choose wholemeal bread, no sauces, water instead of soft drink, and limit frequency to no more than once per fortnight.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried nuggets generate advanced glycation end products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; processed deli meats contain sodium nitrates; nightshade vegetables (tomato, green pepper) may exacerbate symptoms in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute deep-fried nuggets with grilled chicken, avoid or reduce processed deli meats, and consider omitting nightshade toppings if symptom-sensitive.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Cold Cut Trio 6-inch Sandwich with Chicken Nuggets Meal is a Western-style fast-food set meal sold through GrabFood in Singapore. It consists of a 6-inch Subway Cold Cut Trio submarine sandwich — featuring three turkey-based deli meats (turkey bologna, ham, and salami) layered on Italian white bread with standard vegetable toppings including lettuce, tomato, cucumber, green pepper, and onion — served alongside 5 pieces of deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets and one regular sugar-sweetened soft drink. The sandwich is assembled cold; the nuggets are deep-fried, yielding a crispy exterior and moist interior. The meal is halal-certified. Nutritionally, the 650 g set meal delivers approximately 850 kcal, 45 g protein, 35 g fat, and 90 g carbohydrate. Sodium is high at approximately 1,800 mg per serving, driven by the processed deli meats and bread. Dietary fibre is low at 1.23 g per 100 g. The meal carries a low glycemic index (~55) but a high glycemic load (~49.7) per serving due to the large carbohydrate portion from refined bread and soft drink. It is classified as ultra-processed. Clinically, this meal is of high concern for hypertension (very high sodium), type 2 diabetes (high GL, low fibre, SSB), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, sodium nitrates from processed meats). It is not suitable for individuals managing any of these conditions without significant modification.

confidence_reason: Input nutrient values are ai_generated with confidence 0.7 from GrabFood (a delivery platform, which is a forbidden primary source). Values have been cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Subway Cold Cut Trio 6-inch sandwich and are consistent within expected ranges for this meal type. No exact match was found in Singapore HPB Foodo or USDA for this specific composite meal. Confidence is therefore medium: values are plausible and USDA-aligned but not directly verified from a primary approved source for this exact composite.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite meal (sandwich + nuggets + drink) is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. 2. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this specific composite meal is not reported in available approved sources. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite meal are not available in approved sources at the required granularity. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate weighted from white bread, soft drink, and deep-fried chicken nuggets components using University of Sydney GI Database reference values; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite meal. 5. Input source listed as 'https://foodo.sg/' with HPB branding but supporting only 'menu_listing'; nutrient values were ai_generated from GrabFood data. Primary nutrient evidence has been re-anchored to USDA FoodData Central as the approved source. 6. trans_fat_g of 0.08 g/100 g is retained from input; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with deep-fried processed meat products.

Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to align with glycemic_index 55, which is typically considered low GI.
